sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Only row 13 changed (W → B), which is the timer/HUD bar. The board state didn't change! This means clicking magenta at (56, 27) when the square is already at the end doesn't do anything meaningful.
Looking at the objects near the end:
- Gray path segment at (52, 1) area
- White pixels at (52-54, 28-30) - the white diamond
- Purple pixels (21 pixels) at (54-57, 26-29) - the purple square
- Magenta at (56, 27) - center of square
- Dark gray (G) at (59, 25-27) - the end pixel
The square is at the end. The level isn't complete. I need to figure out what to do next. 
Looking back at the earlier pattern:
1. I clicked the initial white diamond at (36, 7) → purple ring appeared
2. I clicked purple ring at (21, 39) → square moved down
3. I clicked magenta centers repeatedly → square moved down each time
4. Now square is at the end
Maybe I need to click the end pixel (G at 59, 27) to complete the level? Or maybe I need to click the purple ring that's still at (21, 39)?
Wait - let me check if the purple ring is still there. Looking at the image, I can see the purple ring at the top. Let me try clicking the end pixel (G) instead.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (revised):
- **Purple ring**: Still at ~(21,39) - haven't tried clicking it again
- **Purple square with magenta**: At ~(56,27) - at the end of path
- **Dark gray end pixel**: At ~(59,27) - the target
- Clicking magenta at end did nothing (only timer changed)
Goal model: Try clicking the end pixel (G) now that square reached it
Plan: Click the dark gray end pixel at (59, 27)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking the end pixel (G)
predict(expect_change=True, note='Click end pixel now that square reached it')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 59, 'col': 27}])
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")

# Check diff
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame
before_ascii = before.ascii.split('\n')
after_ascii = after.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== DIFF ===")
for i in range(64):
    if before_ascii[i] != after_ascii[i]:
        for j in range(64):
            if before_ascii[i][j] != after_ascii[i][j]:
                start = j
                break
        for j in range(63, -1, -1):
            if before_ascii[i][j] != after_ascii[i][j]:
                end = j
                break
        print(f"Row {i}: '{before_ascii[i][start:end+1]}' -> '{after_ascii[i][start:end+1]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
